pattern: Reverse the richer relation into a directed graph, then a memoized depth-first search lets each person inherit the quietest answer reachable among everyone at least as rich.
hints: For each person, the candidates are themselves plus everyone who is definitely at least as rich, so think about the chain of "richer than" relations leading toward them.
Build edges pointing from a richer person to a poorer one; the answer for a person is the quietest result among that person and the people who reach them.
Memoize: answer(x) is x unless some person y richer than x has a quieter best answer, in which case answer(x) inherits answer(y). Cache each computed result to keep it linear.

edge_inputs.description: chains and branches where quiet inheritance flows down several levels
single person with no relations answers themselves
no relations means everyone answers themselves
a richer person who is louder must not override a quieter poorer one
richer person is quieter so the poorer one inherits
single rich source dominating a fan-out of poorer people
linear chain so the richest quiet person propagates to all

stress.description: random consistent DAG built by only adding edges from lower to higher index, guaranteeing no cycles
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 200))
    quiet = list(range(n))
    rng.shuffle(quiet)
    richer = []
    seen = set()
    # edge a->b means a richer than b; only allow a<b to stay acyclic
    pairs = max(0, n - 1)
    for _ in range(pairs):
        a = rng.randint(0, n - 2)
        b = rng.randint(a + 1, n - 1)
        if (a, b) not in seen:
            seen.add((a, b))
            richer.append([a, b])
    return [richer, quiet]
stress.note: constraining a<b keeps the richer relation logically consistent (acyclic)
